items.title: Sources: Benzema 'disrespected' by Al Ittihad offe...
items.summary: Karim Benzema has felt disrespected after the contract extension offered to him by Al Ittihad, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Poch: WC ticket prices nothing to do with USMNT
items.summary: United States coach Mauricio Pochettino said it was not his job -- nor that of his players -- to evaluate or weigh in on the price of tickets for this summer's World Cup.

items.title: USWNT legend Dunn announces retirement at 33
items.summary: United States women's national team great Crystal Dunn on Thursday announced her retirement from soccer at age 33.

items.title: Villa storm into UEL R16; Roma secure top-8 spot
items.summary: Lyon and Aston Villa finished at the top of the Europa League standings, while Roma and Porto narrowly secured a place in the top eight to earn direct passage to the round of 16.

items.title: How MLS' USMNTers endure long offseason to stay sh...
items.summary: This summer's World Cup will be the pinnacle for many USMNT players. For those in MLS, will the long winter offseason complicate their preparation?

items.title: Journalist dreams & Keane reunion? How O'Neill continues to revive Celtic
items.summary: Showman Martin O'Neill continues to thrive as Celtic's interim manager as he guides the club through to the Europa League play-off round.

items.title: Rangers' Euro distraction fades as mammoth month comes into focus
items.summary: Now Rangers are out of Europe, has the road to their domestic path been cleared?

items.title: Premier League: 10 things to look out for this weekend
items.summary: Nico O’Reilly gives Manchester City options, Sunderland miss Granit Xhaka’s grit and West Ham find a way Just when Brighton supporters were hoping their side was building some momentum after a run of five games undefeated in all competitions, Saturday’s stoppage-time loss to Fulham arrived. Fabian Hürzeler’s side led at half-time, but their collapse was typical of performances on the road this season. Securing only two away wins – against Chelsea in September and Nottingham Forest in November – has undermined their challenge for Europe. Their next two games at the Amex, against Everton on Saturday and arch-rivals Crystal Palace next week, are an opportunity to make up some ground. Only seven points separate them from Chelsea in fifth place, but Brighton’s record against David Moyes’s side at home is terrible, having failed to beat them since 2019 when a late Lucas Digne own goal sealed the points for Graham Potter against an Everton team managed by Marco Silva. Ed Aarons Brighton v Everton, Saturday 3pm (all times GMT) Leeds v Arsenal, Saturday 3pm Wolves v Bournemouth, Saturday 3pm Chelsea v West Ham, Saturday 5.30pm Continue reading...

items.title: Boel plaatst uitschakeling Nederlandse clubs in perspectief
items.summary: Alle Nederlandse clubs liggen uit de Europa League. Go Ahead Eagles-trainer Melvin Boel vindt niet dat zijn ploeg de Nederlandse eer hoog moet houden, maar vindt over het algemeen dat het verschil tussen de Nederlandse clubs en de rest niet al te groot is.

items.title: Who is 'shy and nervous' Villa match-winner Jimoh-Aloba?
items.summary: Aston Villa match-winner Jamaldeen Jimoh-Aloba is described as "shy and nervous" by Morgan Rogers - but the teenager announced himself on the European stage.

items.title: Wellenreuther over geïmproviseerd Feyenoord: 'Je moet het accepteren'
items.summary: Timon Wellenreuther moest erg wennen aan de geïmproviseerde verdediging tegen Real Betis. Dat zei de keeper van Feyenoord na de verloren Europa League-wedstrijd.

items.title: Van Gerwen dumped out of Masters as Van Veen, Price progress
items.summary: Michael van Gerwen made a shock early exit from the Winmau World Masters, as Gian van Veen and Gerwyn Price were among the players to make winning starts in Milton Keynes.

items.title: What happened in final round of Europa League matches?
items.summary: Your guide to the teams that made the top eight, the 16 clubs in the play-offs and the sides who have been eliminated from the Europa League.

items.title: Dança dos coeficientes in Portugal: 'Het ziet er steeds beter uit voor ons land'
items.summary: Terwijl de Nederlandse ploegen een voor een de aftocht blazen op de Europese podia, wordt in Portugal de coëfficientenpolonaise gelopen. De Portugezen beleven juist een uitzonderlijk goed seizoen, en de nationale media kunnen hun geluk niet op.

items.title: Jans genoten van FC Utrecht in Europa: 'Zou meer van dit soort ervaringen willen'
items.summary: Slechts één punt in de Europa League, maar hoofdtrainer Ron Jans van FC Utrecht verliet strijdbaar het toneel. Hij heeft genoten van het toernooi en richt zich nu op de Eredivisie. 'Er blijft ook niks anders over.'

items.title: Go Ahead leert in Europa: 'Dat is het mooiste van dit team'
items.summary: Melvin Boel neemt met opgeheven hoofd afscheid van de Europa League met Go Ahead Eagles. De trainer is trots op zijn ploeg, die zeven punten verzamelde. Het avontuur eindigde donderdag met een doelpuntloos gelijkspel tegen Braga.

items.title: Rangers beaten by Porto to conclude dismal European campaign
items.summary: Rangers’ first-half collapse saw them conclude their dismal Europa League campaign with a 3-1 defeat by Porto in the Estadio do Dragao.

items.title: Europa League roundup: Jimoh-Aloba the hero as Aston Villa hit back
items.summary: Nineteen-year-old fires winner to give Villa 3-2 win Rangers’ poor campaign ends with 3-1 loss to Porto The 19-year-old Jamaldeen Jimoh-Aloba was the hero as Aston Villa came from two goals down to beat Red Bull Salzburg 3-2 at Villa Park, though they lost Ollie Watkins to injury in the first half. Jimoh-Aloba hit the winner with three minutes remaining, tucking Kadan Young’s low cross into the corner to cap a fightback that looked unlikely after a limp showing for an hour. Continue reading...

items.title: O’Neill delight as Celtic ease to Europa League playoff with win over Utrecht
items.summary: Celtic race into 3-0 lead inside 19 minutes and win 4-2 O’Neill’s side now face either Stuttgart or Ferencvaros This was only briefly a little more fraught than it needed to be and looked as if it would be for Celtic. Martin O’Neill can focus on the most important factor in that he has guided the Scottish champions into the Europa league’s playoff phase. Job done, once again, for the effervescent O’Neill. Ferencvaros or Stuttgart lie in wait after Celtic closed in 21st place. Celtic were fully deserving of their win in what unexpectedly developed into an entertaining clash with Utrecht. O’Neill, thought to be a managerial yesterday’s man not so long ago, continues to do his bit for 70-somethings everywhere. The Irishman will know Celtic must improve to make meaningful, further progress in this competition but such detail can wait. Even continuation in the Europa League had looked a long shot at one point. Utrecht will be delighted to see the back of a tournament which yielded just a single point from eight grisly fixtures. Continue reading...

items.title: Nottingham Forest seal playoff spot after Igor Jesus fells Ferencvaros
items.summary: Presumably, come Friday lunchtime, Evangelos Marinakis will have more than one eye on events at the House of European Football, Uefa’s headquarters in Nyon. Not only will Olympiakos discover their Champions League playoff opponents but Nottingham Forest will learn whether they will face Fenerbahce or Panathinaikos, his Greek team’s arch rivals, in the Europa League playoffs. Marinakis, the Forest owner, would be public enemy No 1 if Sean Dyche’s side end up across town in Athens. Continue reading...
